Modelling Notes: Brenda: The proton gradient across the mitochondrial inner membrane strongly stimulates the forward reaction, i.e., the generation of NADPH. Mouse Gene symbol: Bckdha and Bckdhb and Dbt and Dld
Modelling Notes: A deficiency in any of the enzymes of this complex as well as an inhibition of the complex as a whole leads to a buildup of branched-chain amino acids and their harmful derivatives in the body. These accumulations lend a sweet smell to bodily excretions (such as ear wax and urine), leading to a pathology known as maple syrup urine disease.  In humans, 24 copies of E2 arranged in octahedral symmetry form the core of the BCKDC.[7] Non-covalently linked to this polymer of 24 E2 subunits are 12 E1 _2_2 tetramers and 6 E3 homodimers.

Equation: M_h_c + 0.18 M_PMF_c + M_pyr_c --> M_h_m + M_pyr_m + 0.18 M_PMF_m

name: Acetoacetate mitochondrial transport via H+ symport
Description: Pyruvate Carrier - Acetoacetate transport via proton symport
Equation: M_h_c + M_acac_c + 0.18 M_PMF_m --> M_h_m + M_acac_m + 0.18 M_PMF_c
Modelling Notes: Before the pyruvate carrier had been characterised biochemical data showed acetatoacetate transport as well (PMID: 6987111)

name: MitoFerrin carrier
Gene Name: solute carrier family 25 (mitochondrial iron transporter), member 37 or 28

name: Amino acid transporter - L-Alanine
Modelling Notes: Heart known to export alanine. Constraint is arbitrary to prevent unrealistic cycling

name: Urea transport
Modelling Notes: In Recon2 this activity has been assigned to gene AQP9 - but this is not mitochondrial! Transporter77 in old model

name: Fumarate transport
Modelling Notes: No dedicated fumarate transporter in mammals - probably non specific transport by members of the carrier family - dicarboxylate? Concentration gradient will make it irreversible

name: Butyrate mitochondrial transport via proton symport, reversible
Modelling Notes: Recon 2 had this as proton coupled as they thought its was transported by ALC16A1. However, evidence suggests this is plasma membrane only and mitochondrial transporter has yet to be discovered.

name: formate mitochondrial transport
Modelling Notes: Unidentified but formate is very toxic to CIV so an active transport step would make sense.
SLC25A32 had been thought to be a folate transporter but it probably transports FAD.

name: Creatine transport to/from mitochondria via diffusion
Phosphocreatine transport to/from mitochondria via diffusion
Bicarbonate diffusion
Modelling Notes: Has no PMF as likely to be generated within the matrix. Transport step is for modelling purposes
